insight: For Spain, the hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a neutral impact on their momentum, with the team gaining momentum in only half of the breaks, resulting in a mean change of zero. This suggests that the breaks have not significantly disrupted or improved Spain's performance, as they have been able to maintain their momentum in only one out of two instances. When Spain was pressing before a break, they were able to keep their momentum going in half of those situations, but since this only happened twice, it's difficult to draw broader conclusions. Overall, the data indicates that hydration breaks have not been a decisive factor in Spain's performance, with the team's momentum remaining relatively stable across the breaks.
